design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through linear stages defeating waves of enemies and boss encounters using punch, kick, jump, and guard mechanics; accumulate Dragon Power gauge for special attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing positioning and timing against multiple enemies while building power gauge for offensive opportunities; defensive blocking creates arm-grab vulnerability windows
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master martial artist protagonist fighting through criminal underworld as part of Shadow Warriors investigation; choice between two fighting styles (Southern agility-based vs Northern strength-based)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Guard/blocking system with risk-reward arm grab mechanic differentiates from button-mashing beat-em-ups; differentiated protagonist movesets (Billy vs Jimmy) force playstyle commitment; power gauge requires active resource management during combat

difficulty.lives_system: Standard lives system (specific count not documented)
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present (specific mechanics not documented)
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; seven linear stages with boss encounters
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection (Billy/Jimmy) provides different difficulty approaches through distinct fighting abilities; guard timing adds skill-based difficulty scaling

progression.upgrade_system: Dragon Power gauge that fills via held button input; partial power grants variable special attacks (flying hurricane kick at higher levels); full gauge grants temporary attack strength boost
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression linear; boss defeats unlock next stage; enemy weapon drops provide temporary equipment usage
replayability_hooks: Character differentiation encourages replays with Billy vs Jimmy
Different enemy weapon pickups affect tactical approach per stage
Multiplayer cooperative play alternative to single-player

world_building_techniques: Stage theming implies narrative context (casino, airport, dojo, truck, slum, forest, hideout)
Enemy character archetypes (heavyweight boxer Jackson, kick-specialist Steve, twin martial artists Chin brothers) suggest organized opposition
Developer commentary reveals intended narrative (Shadow Warriors investigation, Duke as childhood friend) was cut due to time constraints; retail version lacks plot explanation

boss_design.philosophy: Specialized fighting style bosses with distinct mechanical identities; some recur as sub-bosses in later stages to reinforce their threat; mix of single-opponent and paired-boss encounters
notable_bosses: Steve (Mission 1): Suit-wearing kick specialist; recurs as sub-boss
Jackson (Mission 2): Heavyweight boxing champion with distinct stance; recurs as sub-boss
Chin Ron Foo & Chin Ron Pyo (Mission 3): Twin masters specializing in kicks (Foo) and punches (Pyo); leg-grab counter to player kicks
McGuire (Mission 5): Fat clown character with belly-based blocking mechanic
Carlem (Mission 6): Giant gatekeeper with 'Death Leg' middle kick feared by allies
Duke (Final Boss): Flashy martial arts style; cut content reveals intended depth as childhood friend with investigation narrative

faction_indicators: Williams and Roper: common gang members (yellow vest/camouflage vs white vest/bandanna visual differentiation)
Kenpo gym operated by Chin brothers suggests regional power structure
Jackson framed as 'former heavyweight champion' suggests crossover reputation
danger_signals: Baker enemies always dual-armed with dao blades (cannot be disarmed, unobtainable weapons)
Jeff sub-boss appears early; palette-swapped Billy suggests rival practitioners
Recurring boss reappearances (Steve, Jackson) in later stages indicate escalating threat intensity
Final boss Duke's title treatment suggests apex organization threat
environmental_storytelling: Seven distinct stage locations map criminal operation geography: casino → airport → martial arts dojo → moving truck transport → city slum → forest hideout approach → final headquarters
Kenpo gym setting for Chin brothers boss encounter indicates legitimate martial arts institution compromised
Dojo stage suggests operation infiltration of traditional training spaces
Moving truck stage implies supply/logistics network

sources_used: wikipedia: Super Double Dragon article (gameplay mechanics, character roster, boss descriptions, development commentary by Muneki Ebinuma on cut narrative content)
